revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.comment: 新しいページ：皇子塚古墳（群馬県藤岡市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 皇子塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Ojizuka Kofun (Fujioka), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = |所属 = [[白石古墳群]] |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[藤岡市]]三ツ木（字東原）
（毛野国白石丘陵公園内） |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 15|緯度秒 = 33.72 |経度度 =139|経度分 = 2|経度秒 = 30.10 |ISO = JP-10 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径31m（30m）
高さ6m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = [[装飾大刀]]・弓金具・鉄鏃・耳環・小玉・馬具・[[埴輪]]・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 群馬県指定史跡「皇子塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 皇子塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''皇子塚古墳'''（おうじづかこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[藤岡市]]三ツ木にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[白石古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。群馬県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[ファイル:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|単龍環頭大刀柄頭}}{{small|藤岡歴史館展示。}}]] 群馬県南部、鮎川左岸において舌状に張り出した河岸段丘東端に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。北には[[平井地区1号墳]]が隣接する。[[1988年]]（[[昭和]]63年）・[[2013年|2013]]-[[2014年]]度（[[平成]]25-26年度）に範囲確認調査が実施されている。 墳形は円形で、直径31メートル（または30メートル{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}）・高さ6メートルを測る史跡説明板。。墳丘は4段築成。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]のほか[[円筒埴輪]]・[[形象埴輪]]（人物形・馬形・盾形・靫形・大刀形・翳形・鞆形埴輪など）が検出されている{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。主体部の埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南東方向に開口する。玄室・前室からなる複室構造の石室であり、石室内部からは[[装飾大刀]]の金銅装単龍環頭大刀柄頭のほか弓金具2・鉄鏃・耳環・小玉3・馬具が検出されている{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定され（平井地区1号墳とほぼ同時期）{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}、[[7世紀]]前半までの追葬が認められる。 古墳域は[[1992年]]（平成4年）に群馬県指定史跡に指定された。現在では史跡整備のうえで毛野国白石丘陵公園内で公開されている。 === 来歴 === * [[1988年]]（[[昭和]]63年）、史跡整備に伴う範囲確認調査（藤岡市教育委員会、[[1989年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * [[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）5月15日、群馬県指定史跡に指定。 * [[2013年|2013]]-[[2014年]]度（平成25-26年度）、範囲確認調査（藤岡市教育委員会、[[2015年]]に報告書刊行）。 == 埋葬施設 == 主体部の埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南東方向に開口する。玄室・前室からなる複室構造の石室である。石室の規模は次の通り。 * 石室全長：8.33メートル * 玄室：長さ3.52メートル、奥幅2.1メートル、高さ1.6メートル * 前室：長さ1.89メートル * 羨道：長さ2.64メートル 玄室は凝灰岩の切石の切組手法により、前室・羨道は川原石の乱石積みによって構築される{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。玄室の中央には凝灰岩の間仕切り石3石が据えられて玄室の奥・手前を区画するほか、玄門の間には凝灰岩の梱石が据えられる{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 石室の前庭部では須恵器（提瓶・平瓶）・盾形埴輪が検出されている{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 皇子塚古墳 - 1992年（平成4年）5月15日指定{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf 群馬県内の県指定文化財一覧]}}（群馬県ホームページ）。[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html 皇子塚古墳]（藤岡市ホームページ）。。 == 関連施設 == * 藤岡歴史館（藤岡市白石） - 皇子塚古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（群馬県教育委員会・藤岡市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=志村哲|year=2002|chapter=皇子塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=皇子塚古墳（範囲確認調査報告書IV）|publisher=藤岡市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=皇子塚古墳 -平成25・26年度範囲確認調査報告書-|publisher=藤岡市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[白石古墳群]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html 皇子塚古墳] - 藤岡市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:おうしつかこふん ふしおかし}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:藤岡市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:群馬県指定史跡]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 皇子塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Ojizuka Kofun (Fujioka), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = |所属 = [[白石古墳群]] |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[藤岡市]]三ツ木（字東原）
（毛野国白石丘陵公園内） |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 15|緯度秒 = 33.72 |経度度 =139|経度分 = 2|経度秒 = 30.10 |ISO = JP-10 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径31m（30m）
高さ6m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = [[装飾大刀]]・弓金具・鉄鏃・耳環・小玉・馬具・[[埴輪]]・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 群馬県指定史跡「皇子塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 皇子塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''皇子塚古墳'''（おうじづかこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[藤岡市]]三ツ木にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[白石古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。群馬県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|左に[[平井地区1号墳]]、右に皇子塚古墳}}]] [[ファイル:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|単龍環頭大刀柄頭}}{{small|藤岡歴史館展示。}}]] 群馬県南部、鮎川左岸において舌状に張り出した河岸段丘東端に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。北には[[平井地区1号墳]]が隣接する。[[1988年]]（[[昭和]]63年）・[[2013年|2013]]-[[2014年]]度（[[平成]]25-26年度）に範囲確認調査が実施されている。 墳形は円形で、直径31メートル（または30メートル{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}）・高さ6メートルを測る史跡説明板。。墳丘は4段築成。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]のほか[[円筒埴輪]]・[[形象埴輪]]（人物形・馬形・盾形・靫形・大刀形・翳形・鞆形埴輪など）が検出されている{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。主体部の埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南東方向に開口する。玄室・前室からなる複室構造の石室であり、石室内部からは[[装飾大刀]]の金銅装単龍環頭大刀柄頭のほか弓金具2・鉄鏃・耳環・小玉3・馬具が検出されている{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定され（平井地区1号墳とほぼ同時期）{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}、[[7世紀]]前半までの追葬が認められる。 古墳域は[[1992年]]（平成4年）に群馬県指定史跡に指定された。現在では史跡整備のうえで毛野国白石丘陵公園内で公開されている。 === 来歴 === * [[1938年]]（[[昭和]]13年）の『上毛古墳綜覧』に「平井村580号墳」として登載。 * [[1988年]]（昭和63年）、史跡整備に伴う範囲確認調査（藤岡市教育委員会、[[1989年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * [[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）5月15日、群馬県指定史跡に指定。 * [[2013年|2013]]-[[2014年]]度（平成25-26年度）、範囲確認調査（藤岡市教育委員会、[[2015年]]に報告書刊行）。 == 埋葬施設 == 主体部の埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南東方向に開口する。玄室・前室からなる複室構造の石室である。石室の規模は次の通り。 * 石室全長：8.33メートル * 玄室：長さ3.52メートル、奥幅2.1メートル、高さ1.6メートル * 前室：長さ1.89メートル * 羨道：長さ2.64メートル 玄室は凝灰岩の切石の切組手法により、前室・羨道は川原石の乱石積みによって構築される{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。玄室の中央には凝灰岩の間仕切り石3石が据えられて玄室の奥・手前を区画するほか、玄門の間には凝灰岩の梱石が据えられる{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 石室の前庭部では須恵器（提瓶・平瓶）・盾形埴輪が検出されている{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 皇子塚古墳 - 1992年（平成4年）5月15日指定{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf 群馬県内の県指定文化財一覧]}}（群馬県ホームページ）。[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html 皇子塚古墳]（藤岡市ホームページ）。。 == 関連施設 == * 藤岡歴史館（藤岡市白石） - 皇子塚古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（群馬県教育委員会・藤岡市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=志村哲|year=2002|chapter=皇子塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=皇子塚古墳（範囲確認調査報告書IV）|publisher=藤岡市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=皇子塚古墳 -平成25・26年度範囲確認調査報告書-|publisher=藤岡市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[白石古墳群]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html 皇子塚古墳] - 藤岡市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:おうしつかこふん ふしおかし}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:藤岡市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:群馬県指定史跡]]

[[1988年]]（昭和63年）、史跡整備に伴う範囲確認調査（藤岡市教育委員会、[[1989年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * [[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）5月15日、群馬県指定史跡に指定。 * [[2013年|2013]]-[[2014年]]度（平成25-26年度）、範囲確認調査（藤岡市教育委員会、[[2015年]]に報告書刊行）。 == 埋葬施設 == 主体部の埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南東方向に開口する。玄室・前室からなる複室構造の石室である。石室の規模は次の通り。 * 石室全長：8.33メートル * 玄室：長さ3.52メートル、奥幅2.1メートル、高さ1.6メートル * 前室：長さ1.89メートル * 羨道：長さ2.64メートル 玄室は凝灰岩の切石の切組手法により、前室・羨道は川原石の乱石積みによって構築される{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。玄室の中央には凝灰岩の間仕切り石3石が据えられて玄室の奥・手前を区画するほか、玄門の間には凝灰岩の梱石が据えられる{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 石室の前庭部では須恵器（提瓶・平瓶）・盾形埴輪が検出されている{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 皇子塚古墳 - 1992年（平成4年）5月15日指定{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf 群馬県内の県指定文化財一覧]}}（群馬県ホームページ）。[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html 皇子塚古墳]（藤岡市ホームページ）。。 == 関連施設 == * 藤岡歴史館（藤岡市白石） - 皇子塚古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 皇子塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Ojizuka Kofun (Fujioka), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = |所属 = [[白石古墳群]] |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[藤岡市]]三ツ木（字東原）
（毛野国白石丘陵公園内） |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 15|緯度秒 = 33.72 |経度度 =139|経度分 = 2|経度秒 = 30.10 |ISO = JP-10 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径31m（30m）

Gunma|label=皇子塚古墳|lat_deg=36|lat_min=15|lat_sec=33.72|lon_deg=139|lon_min=02|lon_sec=30.10|mark=円墳.png|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=群馬県内の位置}} '''皇子塚古墳'''（おうじづかこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[藤岡市]]三ツ木にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[白石古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。群馬県指定[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|左に[[平井地区1号墳]]、右に皇子塚古墳}}]] [[ファイル:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|単龍環頭大刀柄頭}}{{small|藤岡歴史館展示。}}]] 群馬県南部、鮎川左岸において舌状に張り出した河岸段丘東端に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。北には[[平井地区1号墳]]が隣接する。[[1988年]]（[[昭和]]63年）・[[2013年|2013]]-[[2014年]]度（[[平成]]25-26年度）に範囲確認調査が実施されている。 墳形は円形で、直径31メートル（または30メートル{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}）・高さ6メートルを測る史跡説明板。。墳丘は4段築成。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]のほか[[円筒埴輪]]・[[形象埴輪]]（人物形・馬形・盾形・靫形・大刀形・翳形・鞆形埴輪など）が検出されている{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南東方向に開口する。玄室・前室からなる複室構造の石室であり、石室内部からは[[装飾大刀]]の金銅装単龍環頭大刀柄頭のほか弓金具2・鉄鏃・耳環・小玉3・馬具が検出されている{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定され（平井地区1号墳とほぼ同時期）{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}、[[7世紀]]前半までの追葬が認められる。 古墳域は[[1992年]]（平成4年）に群馬県指定史跡に指定された。現在では史跡整備のうえで毛野国白石丘陵公園内で公開されている。 === 来歴 === * [[1938年]]（[[昭和]]13年）の『上毛古墳綜覧』に「平井村580号墳」として登載。 * [[1988年]]（昭和63年）、史跡整備に伴う範囲確認調査（藤岡市教育委員会、[[1989年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * [[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）5月15日、群馬県指定史跡に指定。 * [[2013年|2013]]-[[2014年]]度（平成25-26年度）、範囲確認調査（藤岡市教育委員会、[[2015年]]に報告書刊行）。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南東方向に開口する。玄室・前室からなる複室構造の石室である。石室の規模は次の通り。 * 石室全長：8.33メートル * 玄室：長さ3.52メートル、奥幅2.1メートル、高さ1.6メートル * 前室：長さ1.89メートル * 羨道：長さ2.64メートル 玄室は凝灰岩の切石の切組手法により、前室・羨道は川原石の乱石積みによって構築される{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。玄室の中央には凝灰岩の間仕切り石3石が据えられて玄室の奥・手前を区画するほか、玄門の間には凝灰岩の梱石が据えられる{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 石室の前庭部では須恵器（提瓶・平瓶）・盾形埴輪が検出されている{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 皇子塚古墳 - 1992年（平成4年）5月15日指定{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf （毛野国白石丘陵公園内） |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 15|緯度秒 = 33.72 |経度度 =139|経度分 = 2|経度秒 = 30.10 |ISO = JP-10 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径31m（30m）
高さ6m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = [[装飾大刀]]・弓金具・鉄鏃・耳環・小玉・馬具・[[埴輪]]・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 群馬県指定史跡「皇子塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=皇子塚古墳|lat_deg=36|lat_min=15|lat_sec=33.72|lon_deg=139|lon_min=02|lon_sec=30.10|mark=円墳.png|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=群馬県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|15|33.72|N|139|02|30.10|E}}}} }} '''皇子塚古墳'''（おうじづかこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[藤岡市]]三ツ木にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[白石古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。群馬県指定[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|左に[[平井地区1号墳]]、右に皇子塚古墳}}]] 群馬県南部、鮎川左岸において舌状に張り出した河岸段丘東端に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。北には[[平井地区1号墳]]が隣接する。[[1988年]]（[[昭和]]63年）・[[2013年|2013]]-[[2014年]]度（[[平成]]25-26年度）に範囲確認調査が実施されている。 墳形は円形で、直径31メートル（または30メートル{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}）・高さ6メートルを測る史跡説明板。。墳丘は4段築成。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]のほか[[円筒埴輪]]・[[形象埴輪]]（人物形・馬形・盾形・靫形・大刀形・翳形・鞆形埴輪など）が検出されている{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南東方向に開口する。玄室・前室からなる複室構造の石室であり、石室内部からは[[装飾大刀]]の金銅装単龍環頭大刀柄頭のほか弓金具2・鉄鏃・耳環・小玉3・馬具が検出されている{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定され（平井地区1号墳とほぼ同時期）{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}、[[7世紀]]前半までの追葬が認められる。 古墳域は[[1992年]]（平成4年）に群馬県指定史跡に指定された。現在では史跡整備のうえで毛野国白石丘陵公園内で公開されている。 === 来歴 === * [[1938年]]（[[昭和]]13年）の『上毛古墳綜覧』に「平井村第580号墳」として登載。 * [[1988年]]（昭和63年）、史跡整備に伴う範囲確認調査（藤岡市教育委員会、[[1989年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * [[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）5月15日、群馬県指定史跡に指定。 * [[2013年|2013]]-[[2014年]]度（平成25-26年度）、範囲確認調査（藤岡市教育委員会、[[2015年]]に報告書刊行）。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南東方向に開口する。玄室・前室からなる複室構造の石室である。石室の規模は次の通り。 * 石室全長：8.33メートル * 玄室：長さ3.52メートル、奥幅2.1メートル、高さ1.6メートル * 前室：長さ1.89メートル * 羨道：長さ2.64メートル 玄室は凝灰岩の切石の切組手法により、前室・羨道は川原石の乱石積みによって構築される{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。玄室の中央には凝灰岩の間仕切り石3石が据えられて玄室の奥・手前を区画するほか、玄門の間には凝灰岩の梱石が据えられる{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 石室の前庭部では須恵器（提瓶・平瓶）・盾形埴輪が検出されている{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 ファイル:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|単龍環頭大刀柄頭
{{small|藤岡歴史館展示。}} ファイル:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|盾形埴輪
{{small|藤岡歴史館展示。}} == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 皇子塚古墳 - 1992年（平成4年）5月15日指定{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf 群馬県内の県指定文化財一覧]}}（群馬県ホームページ）。[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html 皇子塚古墳]（藤岡市ホームページ）。。 == 関連施設 == * 藤岡歴史館（藤岡市白石） - 皇子塚古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（群馬県教育委員会・藤岡市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=志村哲|year=2002|chapter=皇子塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=皇子塚古墳（範囲確認調査報告書IV）|publisher=藤岡市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=皇子塚古墳 -平成25・26年度範囲確認調査報告書-|publisher=藤岡市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[白石古墳群]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html 皇子塚古墳] - 藤岡市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:おうしつかこふん ふしおかし}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:藤岡市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:群馬県指定史跡]]

墳丘 |別名 = |所属 = [[白石古墳群]] |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[藤岡市]]三ツ木（字東原）
（毛野国白石丘陵公園内） |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 15|緯度秒 = 33.72 |経度度 =139|経度分 = 2|経度秒 = 30.10 |ISO = JP-10 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径31m（30m）
高さ6m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = [[装飾大刀]]・弓金具・鉄鏃・耳環・小玉・馬具・[[埴輪]]・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 群馬県指定史跡「皇子塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=皇子塚古墳|lat_deg=36|lat_min=15|lat_sec=33.72|lon_deg=139|lon_min=02|lon_sec=30.10|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=群馬県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|15|33.72|N|139|02|30.10|E}}}} }} '''皇子塚古墳'''（おうじづかこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[藤岡市]]三ツ木にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[白石古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。群馬県指定[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|左に[[平井地区1号墳]]、右に皇子塚古墳}}]] 群馬県南部、鮎川左岸において舌状に張り出した河岸段丘東端に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。北には[[平井地区1号墳]]が隣接する。[[1988年]]（[[昭和]]63年）・[[2013年|2013]]-[[2014年]]度（[[平成]]25-26年度）に範囲確認調査が実施されている。 墳形は円形で、直径31メートル（または30メートル{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}）・高さ6メートルを測る史跡説明板。。墳丘は4段築成。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]のほか[[円筒埴輪]]・[[形象埴輪]]（人物形・馬形・盾形・靫形・大刀形・翳形・鞆形埴輪など）が検出されている{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南東方向に開口する。玄室・前室からなる複室構造の石室であり、石室内部からは[[装飾大刀]]の金銅装単龍環頭大刀柄頭のほか弓金具2・鉄鏃・耳環・小玉3・馬具が検出されている{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定され（平井地区1号墳とほぼ同時期）{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}、[[7世紀]]前半までの追葬が認められる。 古墳域は[[1992年]]（平成4年）に群馬県指定史跡に指定された。現在では史跡整備のうえで毛野国白石丘陵公園内で公開されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1938年]]（[[昭和]]13年）の『上毛古墳綜覧』に「平井村第580号墳」として登載。 * [[1988年]]（昭和63年）、史跡整備に伴う範囲確認調査（藤岡市教育委員会、[[1989年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * [[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）5月15日、群馬県指定史跡に指定。 * [[2013年|2013]]-[[2014年]]度（平成25-26年度）、範囲確認調査（藤岡市教育委員会、[[2015年]]に報告書刊行）。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南東方向に開口する。玄室・前室からなる複室構造の石室である。石室の規模は次の通り。 * 石室全長：8.33メートル * 玄室：長さ3.52メートル、奥幅2.1メートル、高さ1.6メートル * 前室：長さ1.89メートル * 羨道：長さ2.64メートル 玄室は凝灰岩の切石の切組手法により、前室・羨道は川原石の乱石積みによって構築される{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。玄室の中央には凝灰岩の間仕切り石3石が据えられて玄室の奥・手前を区画するほか、玄門の間には凝灰岩の梱石が据えられる{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 石室の前庭部では須恵器（提瓶・平瓶）・盾形埴輪が検出されている{{Sfn|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 ファイル:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|単龍環頭大刀柄頭
{{small|藤岡歴史館展示。}} ファイル:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|盾形埴輪
{{small|藤岡歴史館展示。}} == 文化財 == === 群馬県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 皇子塚古墳 - 1992年（平成4年）5月15日指定{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf 群馬県内の県指定文化財一覧]}}（群馬県ホームページ）。[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html 皇子塚古墳]（藤岡市ホームページ）。。 == 関連施設 == * 藤岡歴史館（藤岡市白石） - 皇子塚古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（群馬県教育委員会・藤岡市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=志村哲|year=2002|chapter=皇子塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|皇子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=皇子塚古墳（範囲確認調査報告書IV）|publisher=藤岡市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=皇子塚古墳 -平成25・26年度範囲確認調査報告書-|publisher=藤岡市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[白石古墳群]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html 皇子塚古墳] - 藤岡市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:おうしつかこふん ふしおかし}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:藤岡市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:群馬県指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] burial mound located in Mitsugi, {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round burial mound]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai District No.1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]..."
revision.text:
'''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] burial mound located in Mitsugi, {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round burial mound]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai District No.1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * **Total chamber length:** 8.33 meters * **Main chamber:** 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * **Antechamber:** 1.89 meters long * **Entrance corridor:** 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Designation === * **Historic Site:** * Ōjizuka Kofun (designated May 15, 1992)[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] (Fujioka City official website). == Related Facilities == * **Fujioka History Museum** (Shiraishi, Fujioka City) - Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Ōjizuka Kofun. == History == * **1938:** Listed in the "Jōmō Kofun Sōran" as "Hirai Village No. 580 Kofun." * **1988:** Boundary confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1989 by Fujioka City Board of Education). * **1992:** Designated a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. * **2013–2014:** Further boundary confirmation surveys conducted (report published in 2015 by Fujioka City Board of Education). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article creation}} * Site description panel (Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} == Related Articles == * [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] - Fujioka City official website. {{DEFAULTSORT:Ojizuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]]

'''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] burial mound located in Mitsugi, {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round burial mound]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai District No.1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * **Total chamber length:** 8.33 meters * **Main chamber:** 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * **Antechamber:** 1.89 meters long * **Entrance corridor:** 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Designation === * **Historic Site:** * Ōjizuka Kofun (designated May 15, 1992)[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] (Fujioka City official website). == Related Facilities == * **Fujioka History Museum** (Shiraishi, Fujioka City) - Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Ōjizuka Kofun. == History == * **1938:** Listed in the "Jōmō Kofun Sōran" as "Hirai Village No. 580 Kofun." * **1988:** Boundary confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1989 by Fujioka City Board of Education). * **1992:** Designated a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. * **2013–2014:** Further boundary confirmation surveys conducted (report published in 2015 by Fujioka City Board of Education). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article creation}} * Site description panel (Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} == Related Articles == * [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] - Fujioka City official website. {{DEFAULTSORT:Ojizuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]][[ja:%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E8%97%A4%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]][[ja:&action=edit]]

The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * **Total chamber length:** 8.33 meters * **Main chamber:** 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * **Antechamber:** 1.89 meters long * **Entrance corridor:** 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Designation === * **Historic Site:** * Ōjizuka Kofun (designated May 15, 1992)[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] (Fujioka City official website). == Related Facilities == * **Fujioka History Museum** (Shiraishi, Fujioka City) - Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Ōjizuka Kofun. == History == * **1938:** Listed in the "Jōmō Kofun Sōran" as "Hirai Village No. 580 Kofun." * **1988:** Boundary confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1989 by Fujioka City Board of Education). * **1992:** Designated a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. * **2013–2014:** Further boundary confirmation surveys conducted (report published in 2015 by Fujioka City Board of Education). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article creation}} * Site description panel (Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} == Related Articles == * [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] - Fujioka City official website. {{DEFAULTSORT:Ojizuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[ja:%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E8%97%A4%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[Category:Most recent 100 pages]] [[ja:&action=edit]]

Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel

Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Ōjizuka Kofun. == History == * '''1938:''' Listed in the "Jōmō Kofun Sōran" as "Hirai Village No. 580 Kofun." * '''1988:''' Boundary confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1989 by Fujioka City Board of Education). * '''1992:''' Designated a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. * '''2013–2014:''' Further boundary confirmation surveys conducted (report published in 2015 by Fujioka City Board of Education). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article creation}} * Site description panel (Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} == Related Articles == * [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] - '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] burial mound located in Mitsugi, {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round burial mound]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai District No.1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ''' Ōjizuka Kofun (designated May 15, 1992)[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] (Fujioka City official website). == Related Facilities == * '''Fujioka History Museum''' (Shiraishi, Fujioka City) - Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Ōjizuka Kofun. == History == * '''1938:''' Listed in the "Jōmō Kofun Sōran" as "Hirai Village No. 580 Kofun." * '''1988:''' Boundary confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1989 by Fujioka City Board of Education). * '''1992:''' Designated a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. * '''2013–2014:''' Further boundary confirmation surveys conducted (report published in 2015 by Fujioka City Board of Education). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article creation}} * Site description panel (Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} == Related Articles == * [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] - Fujioka City official website. {{DEFAULTSORT:Ojizuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[ja:%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E8%97%A4%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:Original pages]][[Category:Most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:&action=edit]]

The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa

Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Ōjizuka Kofun. == History == * '''1938:''' Listed in the "Jōmō Kofun Sōran" as "Hirai Village No. 580 Kofun." * '''1988:''' Boundary confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1989 by Fujioka City Board of Education). * '''1992:''' Designated a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. * '''2013–2014:''' Further boundary confirmation surveys conducted (report published in 2015 by Fujioka City Board of Education). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article creation}} * Site description panel (Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} == Related Articles == * [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] - '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] burial mound located in Mitsugi, {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round burial mound]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai District No.1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa

Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Ōjizuka Kofun. == History == * '''1938:''' Listed in the "Jōmō Kofun Sōran" as "Hirai Village No. 580 Kofun." * '''1988:''' Boundary confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1989 by Fujioka City Board of Education). * '''1992:''' Designated a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. * '''2013–2014:''' Further boundary confirmation surveys conducted (report published in 2015 by Fujioka City Board of Education). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article creation}} * Site description panel (Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} == Related Articles == * [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] - The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel

Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Ōjizuka Kofun. == History == * '''1938:''' Listed in the "Jōmō Kofun Sōran" as "Hirai Village No. 580 Kofun." * '''1988:''' Boundary confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1989 by Fujioka City Board of Education). * '''1992:''' Designated a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. * '''2013–2014:''' Further boundary confirmation surveys conducted (report published in 2015 by Fujioka City Board of Education). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article creation}} * Site description panel (Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} == Related Articles == * [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] - '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] burial mound located in Mitsugi, {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round burial mound]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel

Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel

'''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] burial mound located in Mitsugi, {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round burial mound]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel

Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Ōjizuka Kofun. == History == * '''1938:''' Listed in the "Jōmō Kofun Sōran" as "Hirai Village No. 580 Kofun." * '''1988:''' Boundary confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1989 by Fujioka City Board of Education). * '''1992:''' Designated a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. * '''2013–2014:''' Further boundary confirmation surveys conducted (report published in 2015 by Fujioka City Board of Education). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article creation}} * Site description panel (Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} == Related Articles == * [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] - It is part of the [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round burial mound]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa

Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Ōjizuka Kofun. == History == * '''1938:''' Listed in the "Jōmō Kofun Sōran" as "Hirai Village No. 580 Kofun." * '''1988:''' Boundary confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1989 by Fujioka City Board of Education). * '''1992:''' Designated a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. * '''2013–2014:''' Further boundary confirmation surveys conducted (report published in 2015 by Fujioka City Board of Education). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article creation}} * Site description panel (Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} == Related Articles == * [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] - '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] burial mound located in Mitsugi, {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round burial mound]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ''' Ōjizuka Kofun (designated May 15, 1992)[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] (Fujioka City official website). == Related Facilities == * '''Fujioka History Museum''' (Shiraishi, Fujioka City) - Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Ōjizuka Kofun. == History == * '''1938:''' Listed in the "Jōmō Kofun Sōran" as "Hirai Village No. 580 Kofun." * '''1988:''' Boundary confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1989 by Fujioka City Board of Education). * '''1992:''' Designated a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. * '''2013–2014:''' Further boundary confirmation surveys conducted (report published in 2015 by Fujioka City Board of Education). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article creation}} * Site description panel (Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} == Related Articles == * [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] - Fujioka City official website. {{DEFAULTSORT:Ojizuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Gunma Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōjizuka Kofun (Fujioka)-qq]] [[Category:Ōjizuka Kofun (Fujioka)]][[ja:%E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E8%97%A4%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōjizuka Kofun (Fujioka)-qq]] [[Category:Ōjizuka Kofun (Fujioka)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōjizuka Kofun (Fujioka)-qq]] [[Category:Ōjizuka Kofun (Fujioka)]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:&action=edit]]

Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel

'''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] burial mound located in Mitsugi, {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round burial mound]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel

Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel

'''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] burial mound located in Mitsugi, {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round burial mound]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel

The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] burial mound located in Mitsugi, {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round burial mound]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel

Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel

'''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] burial mound located in Mitsugi, {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round burial mound]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel

The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel

'''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] burial mound located in Mitsugi, {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round burial mound]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[Sue ware]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel

'''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] burial mound located in Mitsugi, {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round burial mound]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室).
