It is part of the [[Shiroishi Kofun Group|Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round Kofun]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ''' Ōjizuka Kofun (designated May 15, 1992)[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] (Fujioka City official website). == Related Facilities == * '''Fujioka History Museum''' (Shiraishi, Fujioka City) - Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Ōjizuka Kofun. == History == * '''1938:''' Listed in the "Jōmō Kofun Sōran" as "Hirai Village No. 580 Kofun." * '''1988:''' Boundary confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1989 by Fujioka City Board of Education). * '''1992:''' Designated a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. * '''2013–2014:''' Further boundary confirmation surveys conducted (report published in 2015 by Fujioka City Board of Education). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article creation}} * Site description panel (Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} == Related Articles == * [[Shiroishi Kofun Group|Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] - Fujioka City official website. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)|version=99995685|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ōjizuka Kofun (Fujioka)]] [[sn:E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E8%97%A4%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:Ōjizuka Kofun (Fujioka)]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujioka, Gunma]] {{Commons category|Ōjizuka Kofun}} [[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[ja: 皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] Kofun located in Mitsugi, [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiroishi Kofun Group|Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round Kofun]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa

Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Ōjizuka Kofun. == History == * '''1938:''' Listed in the "Jōmō Kofun Sōran" as "Hirai Village No. 580 Kofun." * '''1988:''' Boundary confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1989 by Fujioka City Board of Education). * '''1992:''' Designated a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. * '''2013–2014:''' Further boundary confirmation surveys conducted (report published in 2015 by Fujioka City Board of Education). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article creation}} * Site description panel (Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} == Related Articles == * [[Shiroishi Kofun Group|Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] - Fujioka City official website. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)|version=99995685|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Ōjizuka Kofun (Fujioka)]] [[sn:E7%9A%87%E5%AD%90%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E8%97%A4%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:Ōjizuka Kofun (Fujioka)]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujioka, Gunma]] [[en:Ōjizuka Kofun (Fujioka)]] {{Commons category|Ōjizuka Kofun}} [[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] [[Category:Missing wikidata]]

It is part of the [[Shiroishi Kofun Group|Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round Kofun]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ''' Ōjizuka Kofun (designated May 15, 1992)[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] (Fujioka City official website). == Related Facilities == * '''Fujioka History Museum''' (Shiraishi, Fujioka City) - Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Ōjizuka Kofun. == History == * '''1938:''' Listed in the "Jōmō Kofun Sōran" as "Hirai Village No. 580 Kofun." * '''1988:''' Boundary confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1989 by Fujioka City Board of Education). * '''1992:''' Designated a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. * '''2013–2014:''' Further boundary confirmation surveys conducted (report published in 2015 by Fujioka City Board of Education). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article creation}} * Site description panel (Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} == Related Articles == * [[Shiroishi Kofun Group|Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] - Fujioka City official website. {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Translated page but missing wikidata]]{{translated page|ja|皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)|version=99995685|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:Ōjizuka Kofun (Fujioka)]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujioka, Gunma]] {{Commons category|Ōjizuka Kofun}} [[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q108127461}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Ōjizuka Kofun]][[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[ja: 皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] Kofun located in Mitsugi, [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiroishi Kofun Group|Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round Kofun]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa

[[sn:commons:Category:Ōjizuka Kofun]][[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[ja: 皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] Kofun located in Mitsugi, [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiroishi Kofun Group|Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round Kofun]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ''' Ōjizuka Kofun (designated May 15, 1992)[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] (Fujioka City official website). == Related Facilities == * '''Fujioka History Museum''' (Shiraishi, Fujioka City) - Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Ōjizuka Kofun. == History == * '''1938:''' Listed in the "Jōmō Kofun Sōran" as "Hirai Village No. 580 Kofun." * '''1988:''' Boundary confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1989 by Fujioka City Board of Education). * '''1992:''' Designated a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. * '''2013–2014:''' Further boundary confirmation surveys conducted (report published in 2015 by Fujioka City Board of Education). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article creation}} * Site description panel (Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} == Related Articles == * [[Shiroishi Kofun Group|Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] - Fujioka City official website. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)|version=99995685|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:Ōjizuka Kofun (Fujioka)]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujioka, Gunma]] {{Commons category|Ōjizuka Kofun}} [[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q108127461}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. [[commons:Category:Ōjizuka Kofun]][[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] [[commons:Category:Ōjizuka Kofun]][[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ōjizuka Kofun]][[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[ja: 皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] Kofun located in Mitsugi, [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiroishi Kofun Group|Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round Kofun]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. [[commons:Category:Ōjizuka Kofun]][[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] [[commons:Category:Ōjizuka Kofun]][[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ōjizuka Kofun]][[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[ja: 皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] Kofun located in Mitsugi, [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiroishi Kofun Group|Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round Kofun]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa

[[co:commons:Category:Ōjizuka Kofun]][[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Ōjizuka Kofun]][[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ōjizuka Kofun]][[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[ja: 皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] Kofun located in Mitsugi, [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiroishi Kofun Group|Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round Kofun]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Ōjizuka Kofun]][[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Ōjizuka Kofun]][[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ōjizuka Kofun]][[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] Kofun located in Mitsugi, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|WD=Q596486|lt=Fujioka}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiroishi Kofun Group|Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round Kofun]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 単龍環頭大刀柄頭.JPG|Gilt bronze single dragon pommel
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ''' Ōjizuka Kofun (designated May 15, 1992)[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] (Fujioka City official website). == Related Facilities == * '''Fujioka History Museum''' (Shiraishi, Fujioka City) - Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Ōjizuka Kofun. == History == * '''1938:''' Listed in the "Jōmō Kofun Sōran" as "Hirai Village No. 580 Kofun." * '''1988:''' Boundary confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1989 by Fujioka City Board of Education). * '''1992:''' Designated a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. * '''2013–2014:''' Further boundary confirmation surveys conducted (report published in 2015 by Fujioka City Board of Education). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article creation}} * Site description panel (Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} == Related Articles == * [[Shiroishi Kofun Group|Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] - Fujioka City official website. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)|version=99995685|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:Ōjizuka Kofun (Fujioka)]] {{Commons category|Ōjizuka Kofun}} [[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q108127461}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefectural Designation === * '''Historic Site:''' ''' Ōjizuka Kofun (designated May 15, 1992)[https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] (Fujioka City official website). == Related Facilities == * '''Fujioka History Museum''' (Shiraishi, Fujioka City) - Houses and displays artifacts excavated from Ōjizuka Kofun. == History == * '''1938:''' Listed in the "Jōmō Kofun Sōran" as "Hirai Village No. 580 Kofun." * '''1988:''' Boundary confirmation survey conducted (report published in 1989 by Fujioka City Board of Education). * '''1992:''' Designated a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. * '''2013–2014:''' Further boundary confirmation surveys conducted (report published in 2015 by Fujioka City Board of Education). == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article creation}} * Site description panel (Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education). * {{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} == Related Articles == * [[Shiroishi Kofun Group|Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.fujioka.gunma.jp/kakuka/f_bunkazai/oudidukakohun.html Ōjizuka Kofun] - Fujioka City official website. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)|version=99995685|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:Ōjizuka Kofun (Fujioka)]] {{Commons category|Ōjizuka Kofun}} [[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q108127461}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujioka, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Ōjizuka Kofun]][[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Ōjizuka Kofun]][[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Ōjizuka Kofun]][[ja:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市)]] '''Ōjizuka Kofun''' (皇子塚古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] Kofun located in Mitsugi, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|WD=Q596486|lt=Fujioka|1=Fujioka, Gunma}}, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the [[Shiroishi Kofun Group|Shiraishi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a [[round Kofun]] (円墳). The kofun is a designated Gunma Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Ojizuka Kofun and Hirai Kofun Group (Fujioka)-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Left: [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun]]; Right: Ōjizuka Kofun}}]] Ōjizuka Kofun was constructed on a tongue-shaped terrace on the eastern edge of the Ayukawa River's left bank in southern Gunma Prefecture.Site description panel, Gunma Prefecture and Fujioka City Board of Education. It is adjacent to the [[Hirai Area No. 1 Kofun|Hirai District No.1 Kofun]] to the north. Excavation surveys for boundary confirmation were conducted in 1988, and again from 2013 to 2014. The mound is circular in shape, measuring approximately 31 meters in diameter (some sources state 30 meters) and 6 meters in height. It features a four-tier construction. The surface of the mound is covered with [[fukiishi]] (stone facing), along with cylindrical and various shaped haniwa, including human, horse, shield, quiver, sword, fan, and bracer shapes.{{Cite book|title=Continued Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|author=Tetsuya Shimura|year=2002|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|isbn=4490105991|ref=Harvard|Ōjizuka Kofun (Continued)|2002}} The burial facility is a dual-chambered [[corridor-style stone chamber]] with entrances facing southeast. Artifacts discovered within the chamber include a decorated sword with a gilt bronze pommel featuring a single dragon motif, two bow fittings, iron arrowheads, earrings, three beads, horse fittings, and various haniwa. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late [[Kofun period]] during the late 6th century, with burials continuing into the early 7th century. The site was designated as a Gunma Prefectural Historic Site on May 15, 1992,{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100155734.pdf Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Gunma Prefecture official website). and it is now part of the Shiraishi Hill Park, where it is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is a corridor-style stone chamber with a dual-chambered structure consisting of a main chamber (玄室) and an antechamber (前室). Key dimensions are as follows: * '''Total chamber length:''' 8.33 meters * '''Main chamber:''' 3.52 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 1.6 meters high * '''Antechamber:''' 1.89 meters long * '''Entrance corridor:''' 2.64 meters long The main chamber is constructed from tuff cut stones using a fitted masonry technique, while the antechamber and entrance corridor use river stones in a rubble masonry style. Artifacts such as {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} jars and flat jars, along with shield-shaped haniwa, were also found in the forecourt area. {{small|Exhibited at Fujioka History Museum.}} File:皇子塚古墳 (藤岡市) 出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|Shield-shaped haniwa
